content.rendered:
حَرْفُ النِّدَآءِ وَحَرْفُ التَّنْبِيهِ

حَرْفُ النِّدَآءِ وَحَرْفُ التَّنْبِيهِ is a phrase (شِبْهُ الجُمْلَةِ) containing a particle of calling and a particle of attention and a definite noun

 

Surah Meaning / Occurrence Aayat
حَرْفُ النِّدَآءِ وَحَرْفُ التَّنْبِيهِ occurs in the holy Quran approximately 150 times
O People, worship your Lord Who has created you and those يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ اعْبُدُوْا رَبَّكُمْ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ وَالَّذِيْنَ
2-21 before you so that you may become God fearing مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقٌوْنَ
2-104
2-153
2-168
2-172
2-178
2-183
2-208
2-254
2-264
2-267
2-278
2-282
3-100
3-102
3-118
O Prophet! when you divorce women divorce them at their prescribed periods يَآ أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ إِذَا طَلَّقْتُمُ النِّسَآءَ فَطَلِّقُوهُنَّ لِعِدَّتِهِنَّ
and count their periods and fear Allah (SWT), your Lord, do not expel them from وَأَحْصُوا العِدَّةَ وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ رَبَّكُمْ لَا تُخْرِجُوهُنَّ مِنْ
their homes and they should not leave except بُيُوتِهِنَّ وَلَا يَخْرُجْنَ إِلَّآ
that they commit a clear immorality, and these are the limits of Allah (SWT) أَنْ يَأْتِيْنَ بِفَاحِشَةٍ مُّبَيِّنَةٍ وَتِلْكَ حُدُوْدُ اللهِ
and who transgresses the limits of Allah (SWT), then indeed he has wronged himself وَمَنْ يَتَعَدَّ حُدُوْدَ اللهِ فَقَدْ ظَلَمَ نَفْسَهُ
65-1 You know not, Allah (SWT) may bring about after that a matter لَا تَدْرِي لَعَلَّ اللهَ يُحْدِثُ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ أَمْرًا
O Prophet! why do you prohibit what Allah (SWT) has made lawful for you يَآ أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ لِمَ تُحَرِّمُ مَآ أَحَلَّ اللهُ لَكَ
66-1 seeking to please your spouses? and Allah (SWT) is Oft-Forgiving, Most-Merciful تَبْتَغِي مَرْضَاتَ أَزْوَاجِكَ وَاللهُ غَفُوْرٌ رَّحِيْمٌ
66-6
66-7
66-8
66-9
73-1 O the one wrapped in garments يَآ أَيُّهَا المُزَّمِّلِ
74-1 O the one who covers himself يَا أَيُّهَا المُدَّثِّرُ
82-6 O man! what has deceived you concerning your Lord? the Most Generous يَآ أَيُّهَا الإِنْسَانُ مَا غَرَّكَ بِرَبِّكَ الكَرِيْمِ
O man! truly you are returning to your Lord, a sure returning يَآ أَيُّهَا الإِنْسَانُ إِنَّكَ كَادِحٌ إِلَى رَبِّكَ كَدْحًا
84-6 then you will be the one who meets Him فَمُلَاقِيْهِ
109-1 Say, O Disbelievers قُلْ يَا أَيُّهَا الكَافِرُوْنَ

content.rendered:
Daily

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
 
It was narrated that Abu Hurairah (RA), said, “the Messenger (SAW) of Allah (SWT) said, ‘look at the one  قَالَ رَسُوْلُ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ
who is at a lower level than you and do not look at the one who is above you, for that may keep you
from scorning the blessing of Allah (SWT)

1. Sahih Muslim 51, the book of Ascetism, chapter , 7430-9-, pg 366

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
 
It was narrated from Al-Agharr Al-Muzani (RA), a companion of prophet (SAW) عَنِ الأَغَرِّ المُزَنِيِّ وَكَانَتْ لَهُ صُحْبَةٌ أَنَّ رَسُوْلَ اللهِ صَلَّى 
that the Messenger of Allah (SWT) said, اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ قَالَ
“Indeed, there is certainly some kind of shadow upon my heart إِنَّهُ لَيُغَانُ عَلَى قَلْبِي 
so, I ask Allah (SWT) for forgiveness one hundred times a day” وَإِنِّي لَأَسْتَغْفِرُ اللهَ فِي اليَوْمِ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ 

2. Sahih Muslim Vol 7, chapter 12, 6858-41-2702, pg 75

I ask Allah (SWT) for forgiveness one hundred times a day“

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
 
(It was narrated) that Abu Hurairah (RA) said, عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ 
“The Messenger of Allah (SWT) said, قَالَ رَسُوْلُ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ
“Whoever repents before the sun rises from its place of setting مَنْ تَابَ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَطْلُعَ الشَّمْسُ مِنْ مَغْرِبِهَا 
Allah (SWT) will accept his repentance” تَابَ اللهُ عَلَيْهِ 

3. Sahih Muslim Vol 7, chapter 12, 6861-43-2703, pg 77

Allah (SWT) will accept his repentance

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
 
It was narrated that Abu Musa said, “We were with the Prophet (SAW) on a journey and عَنْ أَبِي مُوْسَى قَالَ كُنَّا مَعَ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ فِي سَفَرِ
the people started to recite takbir (saying Allahu Akbar) in loud voices. The Prophet (SAW) said, فَجَعَلَ النَّاسُ يَجْهَرُونَ بِالتَّكْبِيْرِ فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ
“O people, be kind to yourselves for you are not calling upon One who is deaf or absent أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ ارْبَعُوا عَلَى أَنْفُسِكُم إِنَّكُم لَيسَ نَضْعُوْنَ أَضَمَّ وَلَا غَآئِبًا
you are calling upon One Who is All Hearing, Ever Near and He is with you” إِنَّكُم تَدْعُونَهُ سَمِيْعًا قَرِيْبًا وَهُوَ مَعَكُم
he said, while I was behind him, and I was saying, “There is no power and no قَالَ وَأَنَاْ خَلْفَهُ وَأَنَاْ أَقُولُ لَا حَوْلَ وَلَا
strength except with Allah (SWT)” he said, “O Abdullah bin Qais, shall I tell you قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللهِ فَقَالَ يَا عَبْدَ اللهِ بْنَ قَيْسٍ أَلَآ أَدُلُّكَ
of one of the treasures of Paradise?” I said, “Yes, O Messenger of Allah (SWT)” عَلَى كَنْزٍ مِنْ كُنُوزِ الجَنَّةِ فَقُلْتُ بَلَى يَا رَسُولَ اللهِ 
he said, “Say, (There is no power and no strength except with Allah SWT)” قَالَ قُلْ لَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللهِ

4. Sahih Muslim Vol 7, chapter 13, 6862-44-2704, pg 77

one of the treasures of Paradise

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
 
It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (RA) that the Messenger of Allah (SWT) said, “Whoever says, ‘None has عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ أَنَّ رَسُوْلَ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ قَالَ مَنْ قَالَ لَا
the right to be worshipped but Allah (SWT) alone with no partner or associate, His is the dominion, to Him إِلَاهَ إِلَّا اللهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيْكَ لَهُ لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ
is praise and He has power over all things’ 100 hundred times in a day, it will be the equivalent of his freeing الحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيْرٌ فِي يَومٍ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ كَانَتْ لَهُ عِدْلَ
one hundred slaves and one hundred good deeds will be recorded for him, and one hundred bad deeds will عَشْرِ رِقَابٍ وَكُتِبَت لَهُ مِائَةُ حَسَنَةٍ وَمُحِيَتْ عَنْهُ مِائَةُ سَيِّئَةٍ
be erased for him and it will be a protection for him against the Satan all day until evening comes and no one وَكَانَتْ لَهُ حِرْزًا مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ يَوْمَهُ ذَلِكَ حَتَّى يُمْسِيَ وَلَمْ يَأْتِ أَحَدٌ
will do anything better than what he has done except one who does more than that and whoever says, ‘Glory بِأَفْضَلَ مِمَّا جَآءَ بِهِ إِلَّا أَحَدٌ عَمِلَ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ وَمَنْ قَالَ سُبْحَانَ اللهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ  
and praise is to Allah (SWT)’ one hundred times in a day, his sins will be erased even if they are like the foam فِي يَوْمٍ مِائَةَ مَرَّةٍ حُطَّتْ خَطَايَاهُ وَلَوْ كَانَتْ مِثْلَ زَبَدِ البَحْرِ 
of the sea”  

5. Sahih Muslim Vol 7, chapter 10, 6842-28-2691, pg 68

Say 100 hundred times in a day, it will be the equivalent of his freeing one hundred slaves and one hundred good deeds will be recorded for him and one hundred bad deeds will be erased for him and it will be a protection for him against the Satan all day until evening comes and no one will do anything better than what he has done except one who does more than that

and whoever says, ‘Glory and praise is to Allah (SWT)’ one hundred times in a day, his sins will be erased even if they are like the foam of the sea

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (RA) that the Messenger of Allah (SWT) said, “whoever says in the morning عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُوْلُ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ مَنْ قَالَ حِيْنَ يُصْبِحُ
and in the evening, ‘Glory and praise is to Allah (SWT)’ one hundred times, no one will come on the day of وَ حِيْنَ يُمْسِي سُبْحَانَ اللهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ مَائَةَ مَرَّةٍ لَمْ يَأْتِ أَحَدٌ يَوْمَ  
Resurrection with anything better than what he has done, except one who said something like what he said القِيَامَةِ بِأَفْضَلَ مِمَّا جَآءَ بِهِ إِلَّا أَحَدٌ قَالَ مِثْلَ مَا قَالَ 
or more than that'” أَوْ زَادَ عَلَيْهِ 

6. Sahih Muslim Vol 7, chapter 10, 6843-29-2692, pg 69

Say in the morning and evening 100 times

ٍ

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
It was narrated that Abu Hurairah (RA) said, “The Messenger of Allah (SWT) said, ‘Two words that are light on the tongue and heavy أَنَّ رَسُوْلَ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ قَالَ
in the scale and beloved to the Most Merciful, Glory and praise be to Allah (SWT) and Glory be to Allah (SWT) the All Mighty'” قَالَ سُبْحَانَ اللهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ ، سُبْحَانَ اللهِ العَظِيْمِ    

7. Sahih Muslim Vol 7, chapter 10, 6846-31-2694, pg 70

Two words that are light on the tongue and heavy in the scale and beloved to the Most Merciful

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
It was narrated that Abu Hurairah (RA) said, “The Messenger of Allah (SWT) said, ‘Saying (Glory is to Allah (SWT) أَنَّ رَسُوْلَ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ قَالَ
praise is to Allah (SWT), none has the right to be worshipped but Allah (SWT) and Allah (SWT) is most great) لَأَنْ أَقُوْلَ سُبْحَانَ اللهِ وَالحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ وَلَا إِلَاهَ إِلَّا اللهُ وَاللهُ أَكْبَرُ    
is dearer to me than everything upon which the sun rises”  

8. Sahih Muslim Vol 7, chapter 10, 6847-32-2695, pg 70

Messenger of Allah (SWT) said, ‘Saying (Glory is to Allah (SWT), praise is to Allah (SWT), none has the right to be worshipped but Allah (SWT) and Allah (SWT) is most great)’ is dearer to me than everything upon which the sun rises“

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
It was narrated that Abdul Aziz bin Suhaib (RA) said, “Qatadah asked Anas (RA), ‘which supplication عَن عَبْدِ العَزِيزِ وَهُوَ ابْنَ صُهَيْبٍ قَالَ سَأَلَ قَتَادَةُ أَنَسًا أَيُّ دَعْوَةٍ
did the Prophet (SAW) say the most?’ he said, ‘The supplication that he said the most was, كَانَ يَدْعُو بِهَا النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ أَكْثَرَ قَالَ كَانَ أَكْثَرُ دَعْوَةٍ يَدْعُو بَهَا يَقُوْلُ
‘O Allah (SWT) give us good in this world and good in the Hereafter and save us from the torment of the Fire'” اللَّهُمَّ آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ 
When Anas (RA) wanted to say a supplication, he would say these words قَالَ وَكَانَ أَنَسٌ إِذَا أَرَادَ أَنْ يَدْعُوَ بِدَعْوَةٍ دَعَا بِهَا 
and when he had a specific need, he would include these words in his supplication فَإِذَا أَرَادَ أَنْ يَدْعُوَ بِدُعَاءِ دَعَا بِهَا فِيهِ 

9. Sahih Muslim Vol 7, chapter 9, 6840-26-2690, pg 67

The supplication that the Messenger of Allah (SWT) said the most was, “O Allah (SWT) give us good in this world and good in the Hereafter and save us from the torment of the fire”

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
Abdullah bin Amr bin Al-As (RA) said that he heard the Messenger of Allah (SWT) say, عَبْدَ اللهِ بْنَ عَمْرِو بْنِ العَاصِ يَقُولُ أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ رَسُوْلَ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ يَقُوْلُ
“The hearts of the sons of Adam (AS) are all إِنَّ قُلُوبَ بَنِي آدَمَ كُلَّهَا    
between two fingers of the Most Merciful, like one heart, بَيْنَ إِصْبَعَيْنِ مِن أَصَابِعِ الرَّحْمَانِ كَقَلْبٍ وَاحِدٍ 
and He directs them as He wills” then the Messenger of Allah (SWT) said, يُصَرِّفُهُ حَيْثُ يَشَآءُ ثُمَّ قَالَ رَسُوْلُ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ 
“O Allah (SWT), Controller of the hearts, direct our hearts to obey You” اللَّاهُمَّ مُصَرِّفَ القُلُوبِ صَرِّفْ قُلُوْبَنَا عَلَى طَاعَتِكَ 
 

10. Sahih Muslim Vol 7, chapter 3, 6750-17-2654, pg 29

Allah (SWT) directs hearts as He wills

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
Anas bin Malik (RA) said, “The Messenger of Allah (SWT) used to say, حَدَّثَنَا أَنَسُ بْنُ مَالِكِ قَالَ كَانَ رَسُوْلُ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ يَقُولَ
“O Allah (SWT) I seek refuge with You from helplessness, laziness, cowardice, old age اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنَ العَجْزِ وَالكَسَلِ وَالجُبْنِ وَالهَرَمِ
and miserliness and I seek refuge with You from the torment of the grave وَالبُخْلِ وَأَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ عَذَابِ القَبْرِ  
and from the trials of life and death” وَمِنْ فِتْنَةِ المَحْيَا وَالمَمَاتِ 

11. Sahih Muslim Vol 7, chapter 15, 6873-50-2706, pg 81

Messenger of Allah (SWT) used to say

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
It was narrated that Zaid bin Arqam (RA) said, “I do not tell you anything but, that which the Messenger عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ أَرْقَمَ قَالَ لَا أَقُوْلُ لَكُمْ إِلَّا كَمَا كَانَ رَسُوْلُ 
of Allah (SWT) said, he used to say, ‘O Allah! I seek refuge with You from helplessness, laziness اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ يَقُولَ قَالَ كَانَ يَقُوْلُ اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنَ العَجْزِ وَالكَسَلِ
cowardice, miserliness, old age and the torment of the grave, O Allah (SWT) grant my soul piety and وَالجُبْنِ وَالبُخْلِ وَالهَرَمِ وَعَذَابِ القَبْرِ أَللَّهُمَّ آتِ نَفْسِي تَقْوَاهَا
purify it, for You are the best to purify it, You are its Guardian and its Lord, O Allah! I seek refuge with وَزَكَّهَا أَنْتَ خَيْرُ مَنْ زَكَّاهَا أَنْتَ وَلِيُّهَا وَمَوْلَاهَا اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوْذُ بِكَ
You from knowledge that is not beneficial, a heart that is not humble, a soul that is not satisfied مِنْ عِلْمٍ لَا يَنْفَعُ وَمِنْ قَلْبٍ لَا يَخْشَعُ وَمِنْ نَفْسٍ لَا تَشْبَعُ 
and a prayer that is not answered'” وَمِنْ دَعْوَةٍ لَا يُسْتَجَابُ لَهَا 

12. Sahih Muslim Vol 7, chapter 17, 6906-73-2722, pg 95

Messenger of Allah (SWT) used to say

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
It was narrated that Anas (RA) said, the Prophet (SAW) used to say this supplication, “O Allah (SWT) I seek refuge أَنَّ رَسُوْلَ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ قَالَ اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوْذُ
with You from miserliness, laziness, utter senility, the torment of the grave and the trials of life and death” بِكَ مِنَ البُخْلِي وَالكَسَلِ وَأَرْذَلِ الْعُمُرِ وَعَذَابِ القَبْرِ وَفِتْنَةِ المَحْيَا وَالمَمَاتِ 

13. Sahih Muslim Vol 7, chapter 15, 6876-52, pg 82

Prophet (SAW) used to say this supplication

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
It was narrated that Farwah bin Nawfal Al-Ashia’a said, “I asked Aishah (RA) about how the Messenger of عَنْ فَرْوَةَ بْنِ نَوْفَلِ الأَشْجَعِيَّ قَالَ سَأَلْتُ عَائِشَةَ عَمَّا كَانَ رَسُوْلُ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ
Allah (SWT) used to call upon Allah (SWT) in supplication and she said, ‘he used to say, O Allah (SWT), I وَسَلِّمُ يَدْعُو بِهِ اللهَ قَالَتْ كَانَ يَقُوْلُ أَللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي
seek refuge with you from the evil of that which I have done and from the evil of that which I have not done”  أَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِن شَرِّ مَا عَمِلْتُ وَمِنْ شَرِّ مَا لَمْ أَعْمَلْ

14. Sahih Muslim Vol 7, chapter 18, 6895-65-2716, pg 91

The Messenger of Allah (SWT) used to say

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
It was narrated from Abdullah (RA) that the Prophet (SAW) used to say, عَنْ عَبْدِ اللهِ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُوْلُ
“O Allah (SWT) I ask You for guidance, piety, abstinence and independence of means” اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ الهُدَى وَالتُّقَي وَالعَفَافَ وَالغِنَى     

15. Sahih Muslim Vol 7, chapter 17, 6904-72-2721, pg 94

Prophet (SAW) used to say, “O Allah (SWT) I ask You for guidance, piety, abstinence and independence of means”

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
It was narrated that Abu Hurairah (RA) said, “The Messenger of Allah (SWT) used to say, عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ كَانَ رَسُوْلُ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ يَقُوْلُ
“O Allah (SWT) set right for me my religious commitment, which is the safeguard of my affairs اللَّهُمَّ أَصْلِحْ لِي دِيْنِي الَّذِي هُوَ عِصْمَةُ أَمْرِي 
set right for me my worldly affairs in which is my living وَأَصْلِحْ لِي دُنْيَايَ الَّتِي فِيْهَا مَعَاشِي 
set right for me my hereafter in which will be my final abode وَأَصْلِحْ لِي آخِرَتِي الَّتِي فِيهَا مَعَادِي     
Make this life a means of increase in all that is good and make death a relief for me from all evil” وَاجْعَلِ الحَيَاةَ زِيَادَةَ لِي فِي كُلِّ خَيْرٍ وَاجْعَلِ المَوْتَ رَاحَةً لِي مِنْ كُلِّ شَرٍّ 

16. Sahih Muslim Vol 7, chapter 17, 6903-71-2720, pg 94

The Messenger of Allah (SWT) used to say

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
Supplications of Prophet (SAW)   أَنَّ رَسُوْلَ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ قَالَ
“O Allah (SWT) I seek refuge with You from the withdrawing of Your blessing and the loss of health and the sudden onset of Your wrath and anything that may lead to Your displeasure”   قَالَ اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ زَوَالِ نِعْمَتِكَ وَتَحَوُّلِ عَافِيَتِكَ وَفُجَاءَةِ نِقْمَتِكَ وَجَمِيعِ سَخَطِكَ    

17. Sahih Muslim Vol 7, chapter 26, 6944-96-2739, pg 111

It was narrated that Abdullah bin Umar (RA) said, “Among the supplications of the Messenger of Allah (SWT) was, “O Allah (SWT) I seek refuge with You from the withdrawing of Your blessing and the loss of health and the sudden onset of Your wrath and anything that may lead to Your displeasure”

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
Sa’d bin Abi Waqqas (RA) said, I heard Khawlah bint Hakim As-Sulamiyyah say, سَمِعْتَ سَعْدَ أَبِي وَقَّاصٍ يَقُولُ سَمِعْتُخَوْلَةَ بِنْتَ حَكِيْمٍ السُّلَمِيَّةَ تَقُولُ 
“I heard the Messenger of Allah (SWT) say, ‘Whoever stops at a place and says, سَمِعْتَ رَسُوْلَ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ يَقُولُ مَنْ نَزَلَ مَنْزِلًا ثُمَّ قَالَ
I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah (SWT) from the evil of that which He has created أَعُوْذُ بِكَلِماتِ اللهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ   
nothing will harm him until he moves on from that place’ لَمْ يَضُرُّهُ  شَيْءٌ حَتَّى يَرْتَحِلَ مِنْ مَنْزِلِهِ ذَلِكَ 

18. Sahih Muslim Vol 7, chapter 15, 6878-54-2708, pg 83

The Messenger of Allah  (SWT) said, ‘nothing will harm him until he moves on from that place’

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
(It was narrated) from Aishah (RA) that the Messenger of Allah (SWT) عَنْ عَآئِشَةَ أَنَّ رَسُوْلَ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ
used to say these supplications, كَانَ يَدْعُو بِهَآؤُلَآءِ الدَّعَوَاتِ 
“O Allah (SWT) I seek refuge with You from the trial of the fire and the torment of the fire اللَّهُمَّ فَإِنِّي أَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ فِتْنَةِ النَّارِ وَعَذَابِ النَّارِ 
and the trial of the grave and the torment of the grave وَفِتْنَةِ القَبْرِ وَعَذَابِ القَبْرِ 
from the evils of the trial of wealth and from the evils of the trial of poverty وَمِنْ شَرِّ فِتْنَةِ الغِنَى وَمِنْ شَرِّ فِتْنَةِ الفَقْرِ 
and I seek refuge in You from the evil of the trial of the Dajjal وَأَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّ فِتْنَةِ المَسِيحِ الدَّجَّالِ 
O Allah (SWT) wash away my sins with the water of snow and hail اللَّهُمَّ اغْسِلْ خَطَايَايَ بِمَاءِ الثَّلْجِ وَالْبَرَدِ 
and cleanse my heart of sin as a white garment is cleansed of dirt وَنَقِّ قَلْبِي مِنَ الخَطَايَا كَمَا نَقَّيْتَ الثَّوْبَ الأَبْيَضَ مِنَ الدَّنَسِ 
Put a great distance between me and my sins وَبَاعِدْ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَ خَطَايَايَ 
as great as the distance You have made between the east and the west كَمَا بَاعَدْتَ بَيْنَ المَشْرِقِ وَالمَغْرِبِ 
O Allah (SWT) I seek refuge with You from laziness, old age, sin and heavy debt” اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنَ الكَسَلِ وَالهَرَمِ وَالمَأْثَمِ وَالمَغْرَمِ 

19. Sahih Muslim Vol 7, chapter 14, 6871-49-589-sic, pg 80

The Messenger of Allah (SWT) used to say these supplications

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
Abu Malik (RA) narrated that his father heard the Prophet (SAW) say, when a man came to him and said,   أَخْبَرَنَا أَبُو مَالِكٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ وَأَتَاهُ رَجُلٌ فَقَالَ
“O Messenger of Allah (SWT) what should I say when I ask of my Lord?” He said,   يَا رَسُولَ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ كَيْفَ أَقُوْلُ حِيْنَ أَسْأَلُ رَبِّي قَالَ
“O Allah (SWT), forgive me, have mercy on me, keep me safe and sound and grant me provision”   قُلِ اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي وَارْحَمْنِي وَعَافِنِي وَارْزُقْنِي    
and he held his fingers together except the thumb and said, ‘These words sum up for you in this world   وَيَجْمَعُ أَصَابِعَهُ إِلَّا الْإِبْهَامَ فَإِنَّ هَآؤُلَآءِ تَجْمَعُ لَكَ دُنْيَاكَ
and in the Hereafter’   وَالآخِرَتَكَ 

20. Sahih Muslim Vol 7, chapter 10, 6851-36, pg 72

Prophet (SAW) said, “These words sum up for you in this world and the Hereafter”

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
The virtue of tahlil (لَا إِلَاهَ إِلَّا اللهُ) and tasbih (سُبْحَانَ اللهِ)   أَنَّ رَسُوْلَ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ قَالَ
“O Allah (SWT) in Your name I live and in Your name I die” and when he woke up he said, “Praise be to Allah who has given us life after He caused us to die and to Him is the resurrection”   إِذَا أَخَذَ مَضْجَعَهُ قَالَ اللَّهُمَّ بِاسْمِكَ أَحْيَا وَبِاسْمِكَ أَمُوتُ  وَإِذَا اسْتَيْقَظَ قَالَ الحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَحْيَانَا بَعْدَ مَا أَمَاتَنَا وَإِلَيْهِ النُّشُوْرُ    

21. Sahih Muslim Vol 7, chapter 11, 6853-38-2699, pg 73

It was narrated from Al-Bara (RA) that when the Prophet (SAW) went to bed, he said, “O Allah (SWT) in Your name I live and in Your name I die” and when he woke up he said, “Praise be to Allah who has given us life after He caused us to die and to Him is the resurrection”

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
It was narrated that Suhaib (RA) said, “The Messenger (SAW) of Allah (SWT) said, “How wonderful is the case of the believer, قَالَ رَسُوْلُ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ
for all his affairs are good. If something good happens to him, he is thankful for it and that is good for him, if something bad
happens to him, he bears it with patience, and that is good for him. This does not apply to anyone but the believer’”     

22. Sahih Muslim 51, the book of Ascetism, chapter 9 , 7490-56-2994, pg 392

The believer’s affair is all good

In the name of Allah, Most Compassionate, Eternally Merciful بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمَانِ الرَّحِيْمِ
The one who is harsh in arguing   أَنَّ رَسُوْلَ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُ قَالَ
“The most hated of men to Allah (SWT) is the one who is argumentative and is harsh in arguing”   قَالَ رَسُوْلُ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيهِ وَسَلِّمُ إِنَّ أَبْغَضَ الرِّجَالِ إِلَى اللهِ الأَلَدُّ الْخَصِمُ  

23. Sahih Muslim Vol 7, chapter 2, 6780-5-2668, pg 43

It was narrated that Aishah (RA) said, ‘The Messenger of Allah (SWT) said, “The most hated of men to Allah (SWT) is the one who is argumentative and is harsh in arguing”

content.rendered:
إِنَّهُ

إِنَّهُ is a phrase (شِبْهُ الجُمْلَةِ) consisting of an accusative particle of emphasis and an accusative noun

Phrase Particle / Noun Noun   Particle
شِبْهُ الجُمْلَةِ حَرْفُ التَّوْكِيْدِ وَاسْمُ إِنَّ اسْمُ إِنَّ مَنْصُوْبٌ حَرْفُ التَّوْكِيْدِ
Phrase Compound an accusative noun an accusative particle
   
Indeed, He إِنَّهُ = هُ + إِنَّ
Indeed, He He Indeed
Surah/ Below are the 181 verses in the holy Quran containing the emphatic phrase إِنَّهُ
Verse
Then Adam received from his Lord, words and his Lord pardoned him فَتَلَقَّى ءَادَمُ مِنْ رَّبِّهِ كَلِمَاتٍ فَتَابَ عَلَيْهِ
2-37 Truly He is the Acceptor of repentance, Most Merciful إِنَّهُ هُوَ التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيْمُ

74-16 nay! truly he has been towards Our signs, stubborn كَلَّآ إِنَّهُ كَانَ لِآيَاتِنَا عَنِيْدًا
74-18 verily, he thought and plotted إِنَّهُ فَكَّرَ وَقَدَّرَ
74-54 nay! indeed, it is a reminder كَلَّآ إِنَّهُ تَذْكِرَةٌ
79-17 “Go to Firaun, truly he has transgressed” اذْهَبْ إِلَى فِرْعَوْنَ إِنَّهُ طَغَى
81-19 Indeed, it is surely the word of a noble messenger إِنَّهُ لَقَوْلُ رَسُوْلٍ كَرِيْمٍ
84-13 Verily, he was among his people in joy إِنَّهُ كَانَ فِي أَهْلِهِ مَسْرُوْرًا
84-14 Verily, he thought that he would never return (to Us) إِنَّهُ ظَنَّ أَنْ لَّنْ يَحُوْرَ
85-13 Indeed, it is He who originates and repeats إِنَّهُ هُوَ يُبْدِئُ وَيُعِيْدُ
86-8 Truly He is indeed All-Able to return him إِنَّهُ عَلَى رَجْعِهِ لَقَادِرٌ
86-13 truly it is indeed a word decisive إِنَّهُ لَقَوْلٌ فَصْلٌ
87-7 except what Allah (SWT) wills, indeed, He knows the apparent and what is hidden إِلَّا مَا شَآءَ اللهُ إِنَّهُ يَعْلَمُ الجَهْرَ وَمَا يَخْفَى
100-7 and truly He to that he certainly bears witness  وَإِنَّهُ عَلَىٰ ذَٰلِكَ لَشَهِيدٌ 
100-8 and truly he is excessive in his love of wealth وَإِنَّهُ لِحُبِّ الْخَيْرِ لَشَدِيدٌ
110-3 Then glorify the praises of your Nurturer and seek His forgiveness, truly He is oft returning فَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ وَاسْتَغْفِرْهُ إِنَّهُ كَانَ تَوَّابًا

content.rendered:
ب ل غ

Most of the words in the Arabic language are derived from a three-letter root like ب ل غ which represents a core meaning or concept

Below you will find verses of the holy Quran containing words derived from ب ل غ and their meanings

Root

 

Pattern Surah/ Meaning / Occurrence Aayat
Verse The root  ب ل غ  occurs in the holy Quran approximately 77 times
اِسْمٌ 98-1 Not were those who disbelieved from the people of the book and the polytheists  لَمْ يَكُنِ الَّذِيْنَ كَفَرُوْا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ وَالْمُشْرِكِيْنَ مُنْفَكِّيْنَ
to be abandoned until the clear evidence comes to them حَتَّى تَاتِيَهُمُ الْبَيِّنَةُ
98-6
Say, “Obey Allah (SWT) and obey the Messenger but if you turn away then, only upon him قُلْ أَطِيْعُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيْعُوا الرَّسُوْلَ فَإِن تَوَلَّوا فَإِنَّمَا عَلَيْهِ
is what is placed on him and on you is what is placed on you مَا حُمِّلَ وَعَلَيْكُمْ مَا حُمِّلْتُمْ
24-54 and if you obey him, you will be guided and not is on the Messenger except conveying clearly” وَإِن تُطِيْعُوْهُ تَهْتَدُوا وَمَا عَلَى الرَّسُوْلِ إِلَّا البَلَاغُ المُبِيْنُ

content.rendered:
ع ر ف

Most of the words in the Arabic language are derived from a three-letter root like ع ر ف which represents a core meaning or concept

Below you will find verses of the holy Quran containing words derived from ع ر ف and their meanings

Root

 

Pattern Surah/ Meaning / Occurrence Aayat
Verse The root  ع ر ف  occurs in the holy Quran approximately 70 times
اِسْمٌ اسْمٌ 98-1 Not were those who disbelieved from the people of the book and the polytheists  لَمْ يَكُنِ الَّذِيْنَ كَفَرُوْا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ وَالْمُشْرِكِيْنَ مُنْفَكِّيْنَ
to be abandoned until the clear evidence comes to them حَتَّى تَاتِيَهُمُ الْبَيِّنَةُ
98-6
those who if We establish them in the land, they establish the prayer and give the obligatory tax الَّذِيْنَ إِن مَّكَّنَّاهُم فِى الأَرْضِ أَقَامُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَءَاتَوُا الزَّكَاةَ
مَفْعُوْلٌ اسْمٌ 22-41 and they enjoin the right and forbid the wrong and for Allah (SWT) is the end of the matters وَأَمَرُوا بِالمَعْرُوْفِ وَنَهَوا عَنِ المُنكَرِ وَلِلَّهِ عَاقِبَةُ الأُمُورِ

content.rendered:
م ك ن

Most of the words in the Arabic language are derived from a three-letter root like م ك ن which represents a core meaning or concept

Below you will find verses of the holy Quran containing words derived from م ك ن and their meanings

Root

 

Pattern Surah Meaning / Occurrence Aayat
Verse The root  م ك ن  occurs in the holy Quran approximately 18 times
اِسْمٌ 98-1 Not were those who disbelieved from the people of the book and the polytheists  لَمْ يَكُنِ الَّذِيْنَ كَفَرُوْا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ وَالْمُشْرِكِيْنَ مُنْفَكِّيْنَ
to be abandoned until the clear evidence comes to them حَتَّى تَاتِيَهُمُ الْبَيِّنَةُ
98-6
those who if We establish them in the land, they establish the prayer and give the obligatory tax الَّذِيْنَ إِن مَّكَّنَّاهُم فِى الأَرْضِ أَقَامُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَءَاتَوُا الزَّكَاةَ
22-41 and they enjoin the right and forbid the wrong and for Allah (SWT) is the end of the matters وَأَمَرُوا بِالمَعْرُوْفِ وَنَهَوا عَنِ المُنكَرِ وَلِلَّهِ عَاقِبَةُ الأُمُورِ
Allah (SWT) has promised those who believe among you and do righteous deeds وَعَدَ اللهُ الَّذِيْنَ ءَامَنُوا مِنْكُمْ وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ
He will certainly grant them succession in the earth as He gave succession to those who were before them لَيَسْتَخْلِفَنَّهُمْ فِى الأَرْضِ كَمَا اسْتَخْلَفَ الَّذِيْنَ مِن قَبْلِهِم
and that He will surely establish for them their religion which He has approved for them وَلَيُمَكِّنَنَّ لَهُم دِيْنَهُمُ الَّذِي ارْتَضَى لَهُم
and surely, He will exchange for them from after their fear, security for they worship Me, not وَلَيُبَدِّلَنَّهُم مِّن بَعْدِ خَوْفِهِم أَمْنًا يَعْبُدُونَنِى لَا
24-55 they associate with Me anything but whoever disbelieved after that, then those are the defiantly disobedient يُشْرِكُوْنَ بِى شَيْئًا وَمَن كَفَرَ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ فَأُوْلَآئِكَ هُمُ الفَاسِقُوْنَ

content.rendered:
ع ز ز

Most of the words in the Arabic language are derived from a three-letter root like ع ز ز which represents a core meaning or concept

To strengthen, exalt, prevail, be mighty, powerful, strong, noble, illustrious, rare, dear, highly esteemed, precious, become illustrious, exalted.

Below you will find verses of the holy Quran containing words derived from ع ز ز and their meanings

Root

The root ع ز ز occurs approximately 119 times in the holy Quran,

46 times as the nominative noun العَزِيْزُ

0 times as the derived, form 4, active participle المُعِزُّ

 

Surah Meaning Aayat
Verse The root  ع ز ز  occurs in the holy Quran approximately 119 times
“Our Lord, raise from among them a Messenger who will recite to them Your verses رَبَّنَا وَابْعَثْ فِيْهِمْ رَسُوْلًا مِنْهُمْ يَتْلُوْا عَلَيْهِمْ ءَايَاتِكَ
and teach them the book and wisdom and purify them وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ الكِتَابَ وَالحِكْمَةَ وَيُزَكِّيْهِمْ
2-129 Truly You, You are the All-Mighty, the All-Wise إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ العَزِيْزُ الحَكِيْمُ

Those who have been evicted from their homes without right except that they said, “Our Lord is Allah (SWT) الَّذِيْنَ أُخْرِجُوا مِن دِيَارِهِم بِغَيْرِ حَقٍّ إِلَّآ أَن يَقُوْلُوا رَبُّنَا اللهُ
and had not Allah (SWT) checked the people, some of them by others surely would have been demolished وَلَوْلَا دَفْعُ اللهِ النَّاسَ بَعْضَهُم بِبَعْضٍ لَّهُدِّمَتْ
monasteries and churches and synagogues and mosques wherein are mentioned much the name of Allah (SWT) صَوَامِعُ وَبِيَعٌ وَصَلَوَاتٌ وَمَسَاجِدُ يُذْكَرُ فِيْهَا اسْمُ اللهِ كَثِيْرًا
22-40 and Allah (SWT) will surely help who help him indeed, Allah (SWT) is All-Strong, All-Mighty وَلَيَنْصُرَنَّ اللهُ مَن يَنْصُرُهُ إِنَّ اللهَ لَقَوِيٌّ عَزِيْزٌ

59-1 and He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise وَهُوَ العَزِيْزُ الحَكِيْمُ
He is Allah SWT, there is no god except He, the King, the Holy One هُوَ اللهُ الَّذِي لَآ إِلَاهَ إِلَّا هُوَ المَلِكُ القُدُّوْسُ
the Source of Peace, Granter of Security, Guardian over all, the Almighty, the Compeller السَّلَامُ المُؤْمِنُ المُهِيْمِنُ العَزِيْزُ الجَبَّارُ

belonging to Him are the most beautiful names لَهُ الأَسْمَآءُ الحُسْنَى
whatever is in the heavens and the earth glorify Him by it يُسَبِّحُ لَهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأَرْضِ
59-24 and He is the All Mighty, the All Wise وَهُوَ العَزِيْزُ الحَكِيْمُ

content.rendered:
ق و ي

Most of the words in the Arabic language are derived from a three-letter root like ق و ي which represents a core meaning or concept

To be, become strong, prevail, be able to do, be powerful, be vigorous, be forceful

Below you will find verses of the holy Quran containing words derived from ق و ي and their meanings

Root

The root ق و ي occurs approximately 42 times in the holy Quran

3 times as the noun الْقَوِيُّ

7 times as the noun قَوِيٌّ

1 time as the active participle الْمُقْوِيْنَ

ح ل ف

Most of the words in the Arabic language are derived from a three-letter root like ح ل ف which represents a core meaning or concept

Below you will find verses of the holy Quran containing words derived from ح ل ف and their meanings

Root

 

Pattern Surah/ Meaning / Occurrence Aayat
Verse The root  ح ل ف  occurs in the holy Quran approximately 13 times
اِسْمٌ اسْمٌ 4-62
5-89
9-42
9-56
مُضَارِعٌ فِعْلٌ 9-62 They swear by Allah (SWT) to you to please you and Allah (SWT) and His messenger يَحْلِفُوْنَ بِاللهِ لَكُمْ لِيُرْضُوْكُمْ وَاللهُ وَرَسُوْلُهُ
have more right that they should please Him if they are believers أَحَقُّ أَنْ يُرْضُوْهُ إِنْ كَانُوا مُؤْمِنِيْنَ
مُضَارِعٌ فِعْلٌ 9-74 They swear by Allah (SWT) not they said while they certainly said the word of disbelief يَحْلِفُوْنَ بِاللهِ مَا قَالُوا وَلَقَدْ قَالُوا كَلِمَةَ الكُفْرِ
and they disbelieved after their submission and planned for what not they could attain وَكَفَرُوا بَعْدَ إِسْلَامِهِمْ وَهَمُّوا بِمَا لَمْ يَنَالُوا
and not were they resentful except that Allah (SWT) and His Messenger had enriched them وَمَا نَقَمُوا إِلَّا أَنْ اَغْنَاهُمُ اللهُ وَرَسُوْلُهُ
from His bounty so, if they repent it is better for them and if they turn away مِنْ فَضْلِهِ فَإِنْ يَتُوْبُوا يَكُ خَيْرًا لَّهُمْ وَإِنْ يَتَوَلَّوا
Allah (SWT) will punish them with a painful punishment in the world and the hereafter يُعَذِّبْهُمُ اللهُ عَذَابًا أَلِيْمًا فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالئَاخِرَةِ 
and not for them in the earth an ally nor a helper وَمَا لَهُمْ فِي الأَرْضِ مِن وَلِيٍّ وَلَا نَصِيْرٍ

content.rendered:
و د د

Most of the words in the Arabic language are derived from a three-letter root like و د د which represents a core meaning or concept

To love, wish for, desire, be fond of, will, long

Below you will find verses of the holy Quran containing words derived from و د د and their meanings

Root

The root و د د occurs approximately 29 times in the holy Quran

1 time as the noun الْوَدُوْدُ

1 time as the noun وَدُوْدٌ

 

Surah/ Meaning / Occurrence و د د
Verse The root  و د د  occurs approximately 29 times in the holy Quran
And surely you will find them the greediest of mankind for life greedier than the polytheists وَلَتَجِدَنَّهُمْ أَحْرَصَ النَّاسِ عَلَى حَيَاةٍ وَمِنَ الَّذِيْنَ أَشْرَكُوْا
Each one of them loves if he could be granted a life of a thousand years يَوَدُّ أَحَدُهُمْ لَوْ يُعَمَّرُ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ
but it will not remove him from the punishment, the grant of such a life وَمَا هُوَ بِمُزَهْزِحِهِ مِنَ العَذَابِ أَنْ يُعَمَّرَ
2-96 and Allah (SWT) is All-Seeing of what they do وَاللهُ بَصِيْرٌ بِمَا يَعْمَلُوْنَ
Neither those who disbelieve from the people of the book, nor the polytheists wish مَّا يَوَدُّ الَّذِيْنَ كَفَرُوْا مِنْ أَهْلِ الكِتَابِ وَلَا المُشْرِكِيْنَ
that any good be sent down to you from your Lord أَنْ يُنَزَّلَ عَلَيْكُمْ مِنْ خَيْرٍ مِّنْ رَّبِّكُمْ
but Allah (SWT) chooses for His Mercy who He wills وَاللهُ يَخْتَصُّ بِرَحْمَتِهِ مَنْ يَشَآءُ
2-105 and Allah (SWT) is the Possessor of Great Bounty وَاللهُ ذُوْ الفَضْلِ العَظِيْمِ
Many from the people of the book wish they could turn you back from after your faith وَدَّ كَثِيْرٌ مِّنْ أَهْلِ الكِتَابِ لَوْ يَرُدُّوْنَكُمْ مِّنْ بَعْدِ إِيْمَانِكُمْ
to disbelieve, out of envy from themselves after the truth has been made clear to them كُفَّارًا حَسَدًا مِّنْ عِنْدِ أَنْفُسِهِمْ مِّنْ بَعْدِ مَا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الحَقُّ
so, forgive and overlook until Allah (SWT) brings His Command فَاعْفُوْا وَأَصْفَحُوْا حَتَّى يَأْتِيَ اللهُ بِأَمْرِهِ
2-109 Certainly Allah (SWT) is over all things Powerful إِنَّ اللهَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيْرٌ
Would anyone of you wish (love) to be for him a garden of date palms and grape vines أَيَوَدُّ أَحَدُكُمْ أَنْ تَكُوْنَ لَهُ جَنَّةٌ مِّنْ نَخِيْلٍ وَأَعْنَابٍ
flowing from underneath it, the rivers تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الأَنْهَارُ
for him in it from all fruits while old age strikes him لَهُ فِيْهَا مِنْ كُلِّ الثَّمَرَاتِ وَأَصَابَهُ الكِبَرُ
and he has weak children then a whirlwind strikes it, in it a fire, then it is burnt وَلَهُ ذُرِّيَّةٌ ضُعَفَآءُ فَأَصَابَهَآ إِعْصَارٌ فِيْهِ نَارٌ فَأَحْتَرَقَتْ
2-266 like that Allah (SWT) makes clear for you His signs so that you may ponder (think) كَذَلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ اللهُ لَكُمُ الآيَاتِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَفَكَّرُوْنَ
3-30  
3-69  
3-118  
4-42
4-73
4-89
4-102
5-82
and when Allah (SWT) promises you one of the two groups that it for you وَإِذْ يَعِدُكُمُ اللهُ إِحْدَى الطَّآئِفَتَيْنِ أَنَّهَا لَكُمْ
and you wished that other than that of the armed would be for you وَتَوَدُّوْنَ أَنَّ غَيْرَ ذَاتِ الشَّوْكَةِ تَكُوْنُ لَكُمْ
8-7 but Allah (SWT) wished to justify the truth by His words and cut-off the roots of the disbelievers وَيُرِيْدُ اللهُ أَنْ يُحِقَّ الحَقَّ بِكَلِمَاتِهِ وَيَقْطَعَ دَابِرَ الكَافِرِيْنَ
and ask forgiveness of your Lord then turn to Him in repentance وَاسْتَغْفِرُوا رَبَّكُم ثُمَّ تُوبُوا إِلَيهِ
11-90 verily my Lord is Most Merciful, Most Loving إِنَّ رَبِّى رَحِيمٌ وَدُودٌ
15-2
19-96

they will be given sight of them, the criminal will wish يُبَصَّرُنَهُمْ يَوَدُّ المُجْرِمُ
70-11 that he could ransom himself from the punishment of that day by his children لَوْ يَفْتَدِي مِنْ عَذَابِ يَوْمَئِذٍ بِبَنِيْهِ
and they said ‘never leave your gods and never leave Wadd وَقَالُواْ لَا تَذَرُنَّ آلِهَتَكُمْ وَلَا تَذَرُنَّ وَدًّا
71-23 nor Suwa nor Yaguth and Ya’uq qnd Nasr وَلَا سُوَاعًا وَلَا يَغُوْثَ وَيَعُوْقَ وَنَسْرًا
85-14 and He is All-Forgiving, the All-Loving وَهُوَ الغَفُوْرُ الوَدُوْدُ

content.rendered:
و ك ل

Most of the words in the Arabic language are derived from a three-letter root like و ك ل which represents a core meaning or concept

To entrust, confirm, give, charge, dispose affairs, lean upon, rely upon

Below you will find verses of the holy Quran containing words derived from و ك ل and their meanings

Root

The root و ك ل occurs approximately 70 times in the holy Quran

1 time as the noun الْوَكِيْلُ

5 time as the noun وَكِيْلٌ

13 time as the noun وَكِيْلًا

5 time as the noun وَكِيْلٍ

3 times as the active participle المُتَوَكِّلُوْنَ

3 times as the verb 

 

Surah/ The root  و ك ل  occurs approximately 70 times in the holy Quran و ك ل
Verse  
When two parties among you determined that they lost heart, but Allah (SWT) was their protector إِذْ هَمَّتْ طَّآئِفَتَانِ مِنْكُمْ أَنْ تَفْشَلَا وَاللهُ وَلِيُّهُمَا
3-122 and on Allah (SWT) then let the believers put their trust وَعَلَى اللهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ المُؤْمِنُوْنَ
so, by that mercy from Allah (SWT) you dealt gently with them فَبِمَا رَحْمَةٍ مِّنَ اللهِ لِنْتَ لَهُمْ
and had you been rude and harsh hearted surely, they would disperse from around you وَلَوْ كُنْتَ فَظًّا غَلِيْظَ القَلْبِ لَانْفَضُّوا مِنْ حَوْلِكَ
then pardon them and ask forgiveness for them and consult them in the matter فَاعْفُ عَنْهُمْ وَاسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ وَشَاوِرْهُمْ فِيْ الأَمْرِ
then when you have decided then place your trust in Allah (SWT) فَإِذَا عَزَمْتَ فَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ
3-159 Surely, Allah (SWT) loves the ones who place trust (in Him) إِنَّ اللهَ يُحِبُّ المُتَوَكِّلِيْنَ
If Allah (SWT) helps you, then no one can overcome you إِنْ يَنْصُرْكُمُ اللهُ فَلَا غَالِبَ لَكُمْ
and if He forsakes you then who is the one who can help you from after Him? وَإِنْ يَخْذُلْكُمْ فَمَنْ ذَا الَّذِي يَنْصُرُكُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ
3-160 and upon Allah (SWT) then let the believers place their trust وَعَلَى اللهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ المُؤْمِنُوْنَ
those, the people said to them الَّذِيْنَ قَالَ لَهُمُ النَّاسُ
“Verily the people certainly gathered against you, so fear them” إِنَّ النَّاسَ قَدْ جَمَعُوا لَكُمْ فَاخْشَوْهُمْ
but it increased them in faith, and they said, فَزَادَهُمْ إِيْمَانًا وَقَالُوا
3-173 “Sufficient for us is Allah (SWT) and best to entrust (put in charge)” حَسْبُنَا اللهُ وَنِعْمَ الوَكِيْلُ

ن ف ل

Most of the words in the Arabic language are derived from a three-letter root like ن ف ل which represents a core meaning or concept

Below you will find verses of the holy Quran containing words derived from ن ف ل and their meanings

Root

 

Pattern Surah/ Meaning / Occurrence Aayat
Verse The root  ن ف ل  occurs in the holy Quran approximately 4 times
اِسْمٌ اسْمٌ 8-1 They ask you about the spoils of war (the additional) يَسْئَلُوْنَكَ عَنِ الأَنْفَالِ
Say; “The spoils of war belong to Allah (SWT) and the messenger قُلِ الأَنْفَالُ لِلَّهِ وَالرَّسُوْلِ
so, fear Allah (SWT) and correct what is between you فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ وَأَصْلِحُوا ذَاتَ بَيْنِكُمْ
and obey Allah (SWT) and His messenger, if you are believers وَأَطِيْعُوا اللهَ وَرَسُوْلَهُ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِيْنَ
17-79
21-72
